zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de la Roche於大約1120年出生在比利時盧布格拉 洛--亞登時，她的父親Henri I de Namur51歲，母親Mathilde van Limburg43歲。她於大約1145年與Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes在比利時盧布格結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1169年9月10日在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利盧享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de la Roche于大约1120年出生在比利时盧布格拉 洛--亞登时，她的父亲Henri I de Namur51岁，母亲Mathilde van Limburg43岁。她于大约1145年与Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes在比利时盧布格结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1169年9月10日在法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利盧享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de la Roche was born about 1120, in La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgium, her father, Henri I de Namur, was 51 and her mother, Mathilde van Limburg, was 43. She married Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes about 1145, in Luxembourg, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 10 September 1169, in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de la Roche est née environ 1120, à La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgique, son père, Henri I de Namur, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Mathilde van Limburg, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes environ 1145, à Luxembourg, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 septembre 1169, à Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de la Roche um 1120 in La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henri I de Namur 51 und ihre Mutter Mathilde van Limburg 43. Sie hat um 1145 in Luxemburg, Belgien Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. September 1169 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de la Roche è nata il circa 1120 nel La Roche-en-Ardenne, Lussemburgo, Belgio, sua padre, Henri I de Namur, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Mathilde van Limburg, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes circa 1145, nel Lussemburgo, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 10 settembre 1169, nel Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de la Rocheが約1120年にベルギールクセンボウルグラ ロチェ-エン-アルデンで生まれた時，父Henri I de Namurは51歳，母Mathilde van Limburgは43歳であった。約1145年にベルギールクセンボウルグでNicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1169年9月10日にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメロウンで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de la Roche(이)가 대략 1120년 벨기에 루세보우르그 라 록헤--아르데니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henri I de Namur(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilde van Limburg(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 대략 1145년 벨기에 루세보우르그에서 Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1169년 9월 10일 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 루언에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.1157: de 8 de janeiro de 1157 a 7 de janeiro de 1158
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de la Roche nasceu aproximadamente 1120, em La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Henri I de Namur, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde van Limburg, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes aproximadamente 1145, em Luxemburgo, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de setembro de 1169, em Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de la Roche родилась около 1120 г. в местности ла роше-ен-арденн, луксембург, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Henri I de Namur было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Mathilde van Limburg, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes около 1145 г. в местности луксембург, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 10 сентября 1169 г. в местности роуен, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция в возрасте 50 лет.

sm.1157: mai le 8 Ianuari 1157 e o'o I le 7 Ianuari 1158

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de la Roche nació aproximadamente 1120, en La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Henri I de Namur, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Mathilde van Limburg, tenía 43 años. Se casó con Nicholas I D'Oisy Lord of Avesnes aproximadamente 1145, en Luxemburgo, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 10 de septiembre de 1169, en Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 50 años.
